diagnostic_question: Does the current ylong_runtime HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy slow path show same-task IO-readiness wake locality mismatch before any production worker-affinity scheduling patch?
answer: Yes for the diagnostic red gate: the opt-in runtime trace linked IO-readiness task_wake_enqueue -> scheduler_enqueue -> next task_poll_enter for the same task. In a 32 request, concurrency=8, single-client HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy run, 89/89 IO wake enqueue chains completed and 22 chains showed locality mismatch, where the task's last-polled worker differed from the enqueue target and/or next-poll worker. This is red diagnostic evidence only; it does not complete S9F because no worker-affine production candidate or focused/canonical performance matrix has passed.

source_changes.summary: Adds diagnostic-only JSONL trace helper controlled by YLONG_RUNTIME_TRACE_FILE. When unset, record calls return without opening a file.
Wires private runtime_trace module and adds unit test for event JSONL shape.
Records raw waker wake_by_ref task_id and wake_origin when tracing is enabled.
Records task poll enter, pending re-enqueue, and task wake enqueue trace events.
Records local/global/lifo scheduler enqueue context and target worker without changing enqueue policy.
Marks waker wake_by_ref calls made from ScheduleIO::wake0 with wake_origin=io_readiness.
Adds runtime-frontier trace analyzer that links IO wake enqueue chains to scheduler enqueue and next task poll.
Adds analyzer unit test for detecting a cross-worker IO wake chain.

tdd_evidence.expected_failure: error[E0433]: cannot find runtime_trace in crate

tdd_evidence.result: pass: 1 test passed; existing ylong_runtime warnings only

verification_commands.result: pass: 17 benchmark harness tests and 1 runtime-frontier analyzer test

verification_commands.command: YLONG_RUNTIME_TRACE_FILE=$PWD/docs/benchmarks/results/s9f-runtime-frontier-red/runtime-frontier-trace.jsonl python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --bench-bin target/release/https_proxy_bench --scenario https-over-https-proxy --baseline libcurl --requests 32 --warmup 8 --repeats 1 --concurrency 8 --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --phase-timing --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/s9f-runtime-frontier-red

red_gate_interpretation.why: The same task is frequently woken from IO readiness and enqueued/polled on a worker different from its last-polled worker. This directly tests the S9E surface rather than another generic park/yield/steal knob.
limitations: This is a diagnostic, phase-timing, 32-request run; it is not a focused 1000-request candidate matrix and not canonical SOTA evidence.
The trace proves the locality mismatch exists but does not by itself prove a worker-affine scheduling change will improve throughput, p95, CPU, or RSS.
No production worker-affinity scheduling policy has been implemented yet.
red_gate_interpretation.next_action: Implement one behavior-preserving worker-affine IO-readiness scheduling candidate guarded by the diagnostic evidence, then run the S9E focused pass bar before any canonical S9F matrix.
